title: NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) Satellite
focus: Mission objectives, technical features, and launch details of the joint NASA-ISRO Earth observation satellite.
rationale: NISAR is a highly anticipated joint mission with unique technical features, such as being the first dual-band SAR and utilizing a GSLV for a Sun-Synchronous orbit, making it highly examinable for UPSC prelims.
sectionExcerpt: NISAR is a global, microwave imaging mission, with capability to acquire fully polarimetric and interferometric data. It is world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform. NASA provided the L-band radar... ISRO contributed the S-band radar... Launch Vehicle: ISRO’s Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV)-F16. Orbit: Sun Synchronous Polar Orbit. It is the first time a GSLV rocket was used to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous orbit (SSO). Technical Features: SweepSAR... Repeat Cycle: Provides high-resolution data every 12 days.
evidenceBullets: NISAR is the world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band (NASA) and S-band (ISRO) Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR).
It was launched using ISRO's GSLV-F16 into a Sun-Synchronous Polar Orbit (SSO), marking the first time a GSLV rocket was used for an SSO placement.
The mission uses SweepSAR technology to produce high-resolution, wide-swath images and provides high-resolution data every 12 days.
Objectives include measuring woody biomass, tracking crop changes, mapping ice sheets, and characterizing land surface deformation.

title: Gaganyaan Mission and Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS)
focus: India's first human spaceflight programme, its launch vehicle, crew details, and the planned Bharatiya Antariksh Station.
rationale: Human spaceflight is a major milestone for ISRO. Details like the LVM-3 rocket, Vyommitra robot, LEO orbit specifications, and the BAS are prime targets for factual and conceptual questions.
sectionExcerpt: Gaganyaan Programme: It will be India's first Human Space Flight mission. Aim: Demonstration of human spaceflight capability by launching a crew of 3 members to an orbit of 400 km (LEO) for a 3-day mission and bring them back safely to earth. Components of the Gaganyaan: Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM-3): Formerly known as GSLV Mk-III, it is a 3-stage rocket. AI-powered Gynoid (female robot) Vyommitra (half-humanoid) will undertake the first uncrewed mission. Successful launch of Gaganyaan will make India only the 4th country (after the US, Russia, and China) that have launched crewed spacecraft. Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) is India's planned space station... It will orbit around 400 - 450km above the Earth's surface. It will have five modules.
evidenceBullets: Gaganyaan aims to launch a crew of 3 members to a 400 km Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for a 3-day mission.
The mission utilizes the Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM-3), a 3-stage rocket formerly known as GSLV Mk-III.
Vyommitra, an AI-powered half-humanoid (gynoid), will undertake the first uncrewed mission of Gaganyaan.
A successful launch will make India the 4th country to launch a crewed spacecraft, following the US, Russia, and China.
Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) is planned to orbit at 400-450 km above the Earth and will consist of five modules.

title: Aditya-L1 Mission and Solar Exploration
focus: India's first dedicated solar mission, its payloads, the concept of Lagrange Point 1 (L1), and other global solar missions.
rationale: Aditya-L1's recent milestones, the physics of Lagrange points, and specific payloads (VELC, SUIT) are highly relevant for UPSC Science & Technology questions.
sectionExcerpt: About Aditya-L1 Mission: India’s first dedicated solar mission. Objectives: To understand Coronal Heating and Solar Wind Acceleration... 7 payloads: Remote Sensing Payloads: VELC, Solar Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (SUIT), etc. In-situ Payloads: Aditya Solar wind Particle Experiment (ASPEX), etc. It placed in a halo orbit around the Lagrange point 1 (L1) of the Sun-Earth system, which is about 1.5 million km from the Earth. At Lagrange points, the gravitational pull of two large masses precisely equals the centripetal force required for a small object to move with them. These points allow continuous, uninterrupted observation of the Sun. Other Major Solar Missions: Hinotori (ASTRO-A) launched by Japan. Parker Solar Probe launched by US (first spacecraft to fly through the corona).
evidenceBullets: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar mission, placed in a halo orbit around Lagrange point 1 (L1), 1.5 million km from Earth.
It carries 7 payloads, including VELC and SUIT for remote sensing, and ASPEX for in-situ measurements.

Lagrange points are positions where the gravitational pull of two large masses equals the centripetal force required for a small object to move with them, allowing uninterrupted observation and reduced fuel consumption.
The Parker Solar Probe (US) is the first spacecraft to fly through the Sun's corona.
Hinotori (ASTRO-A) is a major solar mission launched by Japan.
